config.name: 12.1-fortress
config.description: Step 12.1 floor-fix bench — Fortress (post-edit) vs Fortress-prefloor (pre-edit) on bulwark-control. 40 games cross-pair (no mirror, no self). Post-edit Fortress passes if it wins >=55% of decisive games. Same tournament harness + caps as 12.0 baseline: 6000 inputs, 150000 events, logLevel=llm.

notes: 1 matchup(s) drew ≥ 40% of games — worth inspecting for stall states.

learningLog: Tournament '12.1-fortress' ran 40 games across 1 matchup(s). Pairs: FortNew=(deck:bulwark-control,bot:experimental/2026-04-19-fortress), FortOld=(deck:bulwark-control,bot:experimental/2026-04-19-fortress-prefloor)

Matchup FortNew vs FortOld: deck 'bulwark-control' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-fortress' versus deck 'bulwark-control' piloted by 'experimental/2026-04-19-fortress-prefloor'. Tied 2-2-36 over 40 games. Same deck, different bots — bots are roughly comparable here, so pick by preference. Average game length: 387 steps.

Across all matchups, 'FortNew' (deck:bulwark-control,bot:experimental/2026-04-19-fortress) outperformed 'FortOld' (deck:bulwark-control,bot:experimental/2026-04-19-fortress-prefloor). Pattern: the top pair's bot profile is worth studying as a candidate to pair with similar decks; the weakest pair suggests either a weight-table mismatch or a deck that disagrees with the bot's scoring priorities.
Observation: 1 matchup(s) drew ≥ 40% of games — worth inspecting for stall states.

LEARNING HINT: When choosing a bot for a new deck, prefer profiles whose matched decks in this tournament share archetype tags (aggro/control/midrange) with the new deck. Avoid pairing a utility bot tuned for defence with an aggro deck — it will underweight tempo plays.
